title: Photon Queue Secures $500,000 Grant to Establish Quantum Memory Operations in New Mexico - Quantum Computing Report
gist: UIUC spinout Photon Queue secured a $500,000 package ($200K non-dilutive New Mexico state grant plus $300K from Roadrunner Venture Studios) to build a room-temperature, cryogenics-free free-space quantum memory verification hub in Albuquerque, part of a broader $1.2M New Mexico quantum infrastructure cohort alongside Mesa Quantum, Conductor Quantum, and UbiQD.

themes.theme: State-level quantum hardware ecosystem funding (New Mexico)

insights.claim: Photon Queue, a 2024 University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign spinout, secured a $500,000 package combining a $200,000 non-dilutive grant from New Mexico's new Quantum Technologies Award program and $300,000 in matching commercialization support from Roadrunner Venture Studios, to build a hub at Roadrunner Quantum Lab in Albuquerque.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: State non-dilutive grant programs plus matching venture-studio capital are becoming a repeatable early-stage funding template for quantum hardware spinouts outside traditional VC hubs.
insights.quote: the capital injection includes a $200,000 non-dilutive grant from the state’s newly minted New Mexico Quantum Technologies Award program, alongside $300,000 in commercialization support and matching capital from Roadrunner Venture Studios

insights.claim: Photon Queue's core technology is a free-space quantum memory that operates using linear optics at room temperature, avoiding the dilution refrigerators, vacuum chambers, and transducer interfaces required by traditional quantum memory approaches, aimed at solving photon timing synchronization across distributed multi-QPU clusters.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A cryogenics-free memory approach could substantially lower the cost and infrastructure footprint of multi-QPU interconnects, a key scaling bottleneck for distributed quantum computing architectures.
insights.quote: Photon Queue addresses this synchronization challenge by developing free-space quantum memories that operate using linear optics at room temperature.

insights.claim: Photon Queue is one of six firms selected for New Mexico's inaugural $1.2 million Technology & Innovation Office funding cohort under the Elevate Quantum Tech Hub, alongside Mesa Quantum (chip-scale atomic clocks and quantum-optimized VCSELs), Conductor Quantum (natural language AI interfaces for system automation), and Los Alamos National Laboratory spin-off UbiQD (quantum dot nanostructures for photonics manufacturing); awardees must maintain active New Mexico operations for at least two years.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Names a cohort of six New Mexico-backed quantum/quantum-adjacent startups to track collectively as a regional ecosystem play, with DARPA benchmarking involvement suggesting defense-relevant technology screening.
insights.quote: Photon Queue is one of six early-stage quantum and quantum-enabling infrastructure firms selected by Economic Development New Mexico’s Technology & Innovation Office (TIO) for its inaugural $1.2 million funding cohort.
insights.locator: Part of a Broad $1.2 Million State Quantum Push
